Risk Description: Claims handlers have used the same manual process for years. Adoption of a new digital workflow may face passive resistance, workarounds, or reversion to legacy practices after go-live.
Mitigation Strategy: Run change impact assessment to identify highest-resistance roles before go-live. Involve two Claims Handlers (who flagged the Excel workarounds) as change champions. Deliver role-specific training using real claim scenarios, not generic system demos. Hold drop-in sessions for the first 3 weeks post-launch.
Contingency Plan: If adoption is below 70% after 4 weeks, implement mandatory digital-only submission cutover and withdraw paper forms from the office

Risk Description: The Sage integration is the most technically complex element of the project. If the API integration fails or is delayed, the manual re-entry process continues, eliminating the largest efficiency saving.
Mitigation Strategy: Require IT Lead to produce Sage integration specification in Sprint 1 before any other development begins. Build and test integration in a Sage sandbox environment before UAT. Schedule integration testing in Week 6 of the 10-week plan.
Contingency Plan: If integration is delayed beyond Week 8, implement a structured CSV import/export as interim data transfer to maintain process flow while integration is resolved

Risk Description: The board approved the Phase 1 scope. However, individual stakeholders may request additional features (mobile app, fraud analytics, third-party integrations) during development and delay the delivery timeline.
Mitigation Strategy: Maintain a formal change control log. Any request not in the approved MoSCoW Phase 1 scope must go through a written change request with impact assessment before being accepted. Operations Director has final approval authority on scope changes.
Contingency Plan: If scope creep threatens the 10-week timeline, escalate to the Board with a revised timeline and cost estimate. Defer non-essential features to Phase 2 roadmap.

Risk Description: Migrating claim history from the legacy system carries risk of data loss, duplication, or corruption — particularly for open claims in progress at the point of cutover.
Mitigation Strategy: Complete a full data audit of the legacy system before migration planning begins. Define data mapping between old and new field structures. Run parallel operation for 2 weeks during cutover — all new claims on digital, all legacy claims processed in both systems.
Contingency Plan: Delay full cutover until data migration validation is complete. Maintain legacy system read-only access for 6 months post-migration for reference.

Risk Description: A proportion of InsureRight claimants are older or less digitally confident and may be unable to use a web-based digital form without assistance.
Mitigation Strategy: Retain a phone submission channel for claimants who cannot use the digital form. Agent completes the digital form on behalf of the claimant during the call. Include accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA) in the platform requirements.
Contingency Plan: If exclusion complaints exceed 10% of submissions in Month 1, investigate and implement assisted digital service or a simplified mobile-first form variant.

Risk Description: The new system will hold personal claimant data and sensitive medical information (for health claims). Any gap in data handling practice could result in a regulatory breach.
Mitigation Strategy: Engage Data Protection Officer in requirements review before build. Ensure Privacy Impact Assessment is completed before go-live. Define data retention periods and automated deletion rules in requirements (IR-017 covers secure messaging). Penetration test the portal before launch.
Contingency Plan: If a breach is identified post-go-live, activate the ICO breach notification procedure within 72 hours as required by GDPR.

Risk Description: If the selected technology supplier is unable to deliver to the agreed 10-week schedule, the transformation timeline slips and the board-approved £320K budget becomes at risk.
Mitigation Strategy: Include milestone-based payment terms in supplier contract. Require weekly progress reports from Week 2. Define clear go/no-go criteria at Week 5 midpoint. Include penalty clauses for milestone slippage in contract negotiation.
Contingency Plan: If supplier is more than 2 weeks behind at the Week 5 checkpoint, assess whether an alternative supplier or in-house build is viable within budget. Escalate to Board.

Risk Description: The board approved the business case on projected savings of £1.09M per year and a payback period under 6 months. If actual adoption or process efficiency does not meet projections, the business case is undermined.
Mitigation Strategy: Define the 5 KPIs from the Benefits Realisation Plan before go-live and assign owners. Measure baseline from the current system in the 4 weeks before cutover. Produce the first monthly performance report at Week 6 post-launch. Review at 3-month and 6-month intervals and report to Board.
Contingency Plan: If KPIs at 3 months are below 60% of target, convene a benefits review with the Operations Director and identify whether it is an adoption issue (people), process issue, or system issue before prescribing a fix.
